content.text: It’s been over a year now since you arrived in the city, and it’s still as vacant as when you first found it. You had hoped to at least find some scavengers, but like the cities before this one, humans appear to be long gone. Maybe it’s time to accept reality.

In the corner of an old street you stumble upon a pile of metal scraps. Empty tin cans, road signs and broken phones. Once again, nothing useful. You dig deeper into the pile when you see something that looks interesting.
An old handheld radio. You pick it up and turn it over in your hands. From the look of it seems to be a two way radio. A little bird is painted on its side, probably hand painted from the look of it.

It’s in surprisingly good condition, and aside from a few scrapes and discoloration it seems like it should be working. The batteries however are completely busted, and no matter what you do it won’t turn on. You put the radio in your bag and start walking. If you remember right, there should be some working batteries at home.

What if there is someone out there and all that can link you to them is this radio? It doesn’t look like much but it is still the best thing you’ve found in a long time. Looking through the rest of the scrap crap does not seem as important now. You are too excited to get the radio working and decide to scurry home to look for those batteries.

The area is like an obstacle course where old lamp posts and debris has fallen. There are few open planes as a lot of the construction still stands tall surrounding what used to be the center of the town. You have grown up in these kind of places and manage to maneuver through the environment
You have made your way to the outskirts of the city where your humble abode lies.

The den is built inside an old bus you found about a year ago. You’ve repaired it with stuff you have found over the years, and you tried to make it as cosy as one can with a few pieces of cloth and metal parts.
As you enter you yell “honey, I’m home” to an old poster depicting a blond young guy.

The guy on the poster is probably named Justin since it says so in big text written all over it. He has been your only company and confidant since you moved here. Not a big talker though. You turn on a blow torch attached to the wall underneath a homemade stow to boil yourself some tea.
You know you had them somewhere around here..
Bang!

You accidentally pushed over a bunch of books. As you pick them up you notice an old picture of you and your dad that must have fallen out from one of them. You really miss him. You haven't seen any of your family in 10 years now. They all have either died or gotten lost in the big forest.
The bookshelf is a cluster of gizmos and “important” papers, but no batteries it seems. You turn to the kitchen area to turn of the blowtorch when you see something in the food rack

Aha! You finally found them. For some reason they were stashed among the food reserve. You eagerly place the batteries in the radio, trying to be as gentle as possible with the old machine. As you hold your breath in anticipation a small light flickers on, indicating your success. It’s working!
Holding the radio close to your mouth… you start to speak.
- “H-hello? Hello? Can anyone hear me?” You are met with silence.

- “Hello?? If there’s is somebody out there, please respond!” Only more silence. Defeated, you put the radio down. It was a long shot to begin with. Who knows how long that thing had been lying there? Whoever owned it is probably long dead. Still… you can’t help but feeling a bit disappointed.
Suddenly, a loud crackles erupt from the small radio
You stand speechless for a few seconds, not daring to believe what you just heard. The voice speaks again.
- “Anybody? Hello?”
It’s the voice of a man. He sounds like someone your own age, maybe in his twenties.
Realizing you still haven’t said anything, you answer him.
- “I’m here! … Who is this?”
Excitement and relief fills the man’s voice
- “Thank god. My name is Axel! Are you alone?”
- “I see…” He sounds disappointed. “Have you seen a woman where you are? In her 40s, short black hair?”
You don’t have to think long on that one.
- “Nope, nobody here. Just me. For a while now.”
- “The radio you’re using, what does it look like?”
- “It seems like a regular two way radio. It has a little bird painted on it.”
- “That’s hers!”
You hesitate a bit before continuing.
- “I, uhm… found it in a pile of scraps...”
Knowing full well what that probably means, you both sit in silence for a while.
- “I’m sorry”, you finally say.
- “It’s alright. I think I’ve known for a while, but I just wasn’t quite ready to give up, you know?”

When he stops talking you feel a pit of worry in your stomach. Is the conversation over? Has he left? What should one say, at a time like this? Your eyes anxiously dart to Justin on the wall. He’d probably know what to say.
The silence is deafening. Without thinking, you blurt out

choices.text: “So uhm… are you by yourself as well?”

content.text: - “Yeah, I am. Hard to find people these days, you know? I can probably count the names of everyone I’ve ever known on my fingers”
He sighs to himself before continuing.
- “Speaking of which, I never asked your name”, he says quietly.
The question catches you off guard. You hadn’t even realized that you forgot to introduce yourself.
- “It’s Abigail”, you answer him, more curtly than planned.
- “Abigail, huh. Can I call you Abby?”
Your prompt answers makes him laugh and it puts you both at more ease. Before you know it you both find yourselves pleasantly stuck in
The two of you talk for an hour about nothing in particular. Scavenging finds, tricky generator problems, where you grew up. Like you, he seems to have lived alone for quite some time.
You remind yourself that you know nothing about this man, but you can’t deny that you’re enjoying the small talk. You may not trust him yet, but you start feeling… comfortable.
- “This is one of the best talks I’ve had in a while”
- “Well it is the only talk you’ve had in a while, so I’m guessing the bar is not that high”
Actually, you and Justin have had some deep conversations in the past, but it’s probably best not to tell him that.
- “We’ve talked for a while now. Maybe we should give the batteries some rest before the radio dies.”
The mention of the radio turns Alex attention.
- “You know, I never asked you this earlier but...where did you find the radio?”
“I definitely have someone here. With me.”
You lower your voice a few octanes
- “Yup, I am a big dude with here with her.”
The radio is silent for a while.
“That was you, you just lowered your voice? Damn.”
A little embarrassed you still stick to your answer. And to be totally fair Justin is right there with you. And he probably would have talked like that.
- “Look, I’m not some bad guy who’s out to get you. I lost my only friend, so I’ve been alone for a long time and I’m really glad to hear another human voice.”
You’re not sure what to think. You’re scared and excited at the same time. It’s been forever since you last talked to someone.
Better be safe than sorry until you figure out who this guy is.

You talk for a little while and you tell him that you have a friend who lives there with you and that he is just out to gather supplies at the moment, making sure not to mention where you live. You learn that he is about your age and that he also have been on his own for quite some time.

choices.text: He doesn't sound all that bad.

content.text: Maybe you were just overreacting. There is not a lot of people around, so why would he lie? You decide to get to know this guy to find out a bit more and hopefully gain a friend. You lift the radio to your mouth once more.
- “Nice to meet you Abigail. Let me introduce myself again from the start. My name is Axel and I am very glad you found that radio. What does it look like?”
- “It seems like a regular two way radio. It has a little bird painted on it.”
- “I knew it! I think it belongs to a friend of mine!”

He tells you that he is looking for his friend, a middle aged woman with short black hair. She was out gathering stuff when she disappeared all of a sudden. They had been talking on a radio on this frequence while she was out.

choices.text: “I’m sorry, but I haven’t seen another person in a long time”

content.text: - ”I thought you said you lived with another person?”
“Oh, umh… kinda” Your voice goes up at the end. Dammit.
- “Is it a dog? Or a cat? You don’t have to worry, seriously. You have no reason not to trust me. There is like, what, 1000 people left in this country? I just think it’s better to stick together.”
“I guess, fine. I’m alone and I just wanted to make sure you weren’t some weirdo you know.. Are you sure you’re not some weirdo?”
He laughs and ensures you that he is not.
“And you’re not just out to find a female to breed with now that we are like the last people on earth?”
- “Well, that would be a plus. But right now I’m just so glad to not be alone anymore. Can we please be friends?”
You decide to trust him for now and loosen up a bit.
“...sure.”

choices.text: “Good. So, where did you find the radio?”

content.text: You stay quiet for a while. Telling him where you found the radio would hint to your location. On the other hand, your base is pretty far away from where you found it. You decide to risk it.
- “I found it at the center of the old capital”
- “The capital? That’s not too far from where I am! Only like a day or so!” he says with excitement in his voice.
- “Hey, I know we don’t know each other, and I get if you don’t trust me just yet, but… why don’t we meet up?”
What?
It’s the one thing you’ve wished to hear for the longest time, but when actually presented with the option to meet another person you find yourself conflicted.
Is it really a good idea? You have no idea who this man is, or what he wants. The risk you’d be taking is massive.
On the other hand… it’s been years since you’ve met someone. Who knows how many people are left in this country? If you turn him down it would mean that you resolve to be alone...indefinitely.
You consider his offer in silence.
- “...Abigail?”, he asks after a while.
When was the last time you heard someone say your name out loud? It’s been so long, you don’t even remember. Hearing the sound of your name, you know what your answer is going to be.
If the alternative is to never hear someone say your name again, it’s worth the risk. No matter how great it is. You gather some courage, and answer him.
- “Ok. Let’s do it.”
You decide to at least give him a chance. This comes as joyous news to Axel who can’t contain his excitement.

- “I’m glad! To be honest I’m afraid of going a little crazy after being alone for so long” You hear the sound of crumpling paper in the background before he continues “Hold on let me just check out the map then I’ll guide you out! Get your things ready in the meanwhile”

You put the radio down and look around your place. Though it’s only been a year, it‘s still a home. But it’s time to leave it behind. Scanning your shelves you look for things you could bring with you. Flashlight, your water container, batteries, food cans, sleeping hammock aaaand Justin of course. Like you would leave him behind.
After carefully placing him at the top of your bag, you turn your attention back to the radio.
- “Axel do you hear me? I’m done. Do you know the way yet?”

- “I hear you. Yeah I think I’ve figured out a good route for you. Head for the center city fountain, and then walk straight from there on the main road out of the city. I’ll give you proper directions from there”

The afternoon sun is shining as you reach the fountain, and you start making your way along the main road. Old cars and road signs are covered in green, plants and flowers of different kinds sticking out through the cracked cement. The passed winter had been gentle, and in the distance you can see a few hares thriving in the fast growing grass.
You find it hard to imagine the city’s past, like it was described in the books and stories.
- “Hey Axel?”
- “Yeah?”
- “Did your parents ever talk about the war?”
- “A bit, my dad was quite invested in it. Most things I know about it are from books though.”

choices.text: How come your Dad was so interested in it?
Books? So you know how to read?
You must know a lot about it

content.text: - “His grandfather, my great grandfather, used to talk about it a lot. He grew up during the last years of the war, around when the last nuclear bombs dropped. It was...not pretty from what I heard. A lot of people died from radiation. It killed him in the end as well. My dad was quite fond of him.”
- “Was?”
- “Yeah, my dad taught me.”

You’re surprised about this discovery. These days kids rarely learned how to read, other than to identify road and store signs. The only reasons you had learned it yourself was because your mom had insisted, and wanted you to be able to use different machine manuals. To find another literate person these days was close to a miracle.
- “So you have read a lot about the war then?”

- “Enough to know the key points at least. The political divide, the bad leadership and greed of the biggest players, the world war declaration and of course the nuclear industrial boom...no pun intended. My dad had huge collection of books and newspapers from back in the day. We used to talk about it alot.”
- “Used to?”
He laughs a bit in response.

- “That would be a bit of an understatement, but yes. I know enough. Mainly the key points, like how the countries turned on one another and how the terrible leadership of the two main players started the third world war. Then the nuclear industrializing started, which was my Dad’s favorite topic. I still remember how his eyes used to trail off as he talked about stories his grandfather told him about when the last bombs dropped throughout the world.”
- “Used to?”
- “Yeah he...he passed away a few years ago.”
- “I’m sorry, I didn’t mean to…”
- “No, no it’s fine, it was years ago. But let’s talk about something else.”
The conversation dies down a bit, and you find yourself unsure what to say. Only a few minutes into a conversation and you’ve already made it awkward. Thankfully Axel picks up where you left up.
- “How come you’re so interested in the war anyway?”

choices.text: It fascinates me, how the world was before
I read a lot about it when I was younger

content.text: You trail of a bit before continuing.

- “It just seems crazy you know? The way people lived their lives. The waste of food, the houses, the trading systems. Even just the small stuff, like their heat and water management! I can’t imagine a functional home without at least 2 generators in it.”
Axel laughs a bit at your last statement
- You do seem to be very invested with generators. I can’t blame you, they’re quite useful.

choices.text: You can’t have too many generators!
I am the Queen of generators

content.text: - “A fellow reader huh?” he says approvingly.”
- “Well technically it’s my mother’s books. She taught me to read and write using them and a few generator manuals.”
Your last statement earns you an impressed whistle from Axel.
- “Generator manuals huh? You do seem to be very invested in those.” he muses “I can’t blame you, they’re quite useful.”

choices.text: You can’t have too many generators!
I am the Queen of generators

content.text: - “You’re not?” he asks confusedly. “You seem to know so much about it?”
- “Well, I do know my history. My mother made me read all kinds of stuff to teach me how to read. History books were a bit lighter than generator manuals.”
Your last statement earns you an impressed whistle from Axel.
- “Generator manuals huh? You do seem to be very invested with those.” he muses “I can’t blame you, they’re quite useful.”

choices.text: You can’t have too many generators!
I am the Queen of generators

content.text: - “Alright, alright you’ve convinced me” he laughs “I give my full respect to generators.”
- “Damn straight. It’s a shame I had to leave my latest one behind. I spent so many hours on that baby.”
- “With your burning passion I bet you can make another one”
Suddenly, his attention shifts.
- “Are you out of the city yet?”
- “In a few minutes, I can see the woods up ahead”
- “Good, the sun is starting to
You don’t want to waste any battery by using the only flashlight you have, and it would be very tricky to tread unknown lands without it, so you decide to make camp before it becomes pitch black.

You throw your homemade hammock up in a tree that seems like a good and sturdy tree. The hammock is sown together with a bunch of old kids bed linen decorated with pictures of cartoon characters, which you find soothing and cute to look at. Then you find a good branch to hang your backpack on and start to cook dinner before bed.
The dinner is a yummy can with conservatives that you heat over a small fire. The dinner is not so much food as it is conservatives since it’s still edible after 150 years~.

You wake up early. The landscape is covered in a vale of mist that slowly swoops and swirls around the trees and vegetation. You managed to get some good shut eye and feel excited about what's to come.
- ”Good morning! You declare into the radio. A somewhat less energetic voice responds”
- ”Mornin! Did you manage to get some sleep?”
- ”Yeah, I must have passed out pretty fast after we stopped talking.”
- ”Good! Today will be a long hike, he chuckles.”
The landscape opens up around you as you get through the thicker vegetation. You gaze down the valley Axel has given you directions to go through.

A distant rumble can be heard from the west. Ever since the ground started to shake in these lands, the landscape changed somewhat and created large cracks and cliffs. The rumble comes from a big waterfall that wasn't there when you came here a year ago. It looks beautiful from here.

Large round discs tower, breaking the horizon. They almost look like enormous metal flowers. You believe they were used to send and receive signals. Beyond the metal discs lie nothing but wasteland for as long as a one day march.
Right across the valley the vegetations grows thicker again and and you can see the little gap that Axel said you should pass through.

choices.text: go down and through the gap.

content.text: As you go through the valley you keep yourself somewhat hidden, just in case. As your father used to say; you never know what’s out there. Looking at flower filled valley you start to wonder.. It is amazing how the greenery recovered so fast from the war compared to the humans. On the other hand, maybe it’s not so strange when people continued to kill each other out of fear or greed when food and supplies became scarce.

You just manage to catch your balance. You look down to see what it was that made you trip only to discover that it is a used fire pit with rocks surrounding a pile of ashes. You gasp when you realize and then become a bit worried. Is there another person here? You have never actually met another person since you were left on your own so you don’t know what to expect.
- “Axeel, psst. Are you there?”
The radio crackles to life.
- “Yes! And.. Why are you whispering?”
- “Because I just stepped on an old camp fire!? Someone has been here or are still here! What should I do? Should I look if someone is still here?”
- “What? Did you see anything else? And where are you?”
- “I am almost through the valley”
- “Oh, I would check, just to be sure. What if the person is nice, or maybe there are some things you could use? You should look.”

choices.text: You look around to see if you find something
You feel like it’s better to move on

content.text: Even though you don't know Axel that well but it feels a bit safer after hearing him say that you should look.You sneak into the surrounding bushes and try to be as quiet as you can. Not making any noise, you wait for a while but the sound around you stays the same. It’s probably safe so you decide to walk around the fire pit to see if you find anything. You can’t find any other signs of life so this was probably someone passing through just like you. You tell Axel, who sounds disappointed at first but then starts to talk about food which makes you both hungry so you decide to capture some. After some effort, you manage to electrify a poor squirrel using a battery device with a trigger that you built yourself. Almost crisp right away so you hardly have to cook the meal afterwards. Most practical in your opinion.
The meeting is getting closer.

choices.text: Time to move on in your given direction.

content.text: You have never actually met another person after being left on your own and you don’t want to risk finding out if it’s a bad guy by being stabbed or robbed when you have this little gear on you. You wait until you’ve put some distance between you and the fire pit until you radio Axel again. He becomes very grumpy after hearing that you decided to leave the site instead of examining it. You feel very surprised by this and feel that he is overreacting for no reason. It is your safety after all. You don’t talk for awhile. but then he contacts you again apologizing for his reaction and pretty shortly after you are already making jokes and feel better again.

choices.text: You have now made your way through the valley

content.text: - “Alright, where do I go now?”
You receive no response.
- “Axel?”
Finally the radio comes to life.
- “I’m here, I’m here. Sorry, I was just thinking about some stuff.”
- “What’s up?”
- “It’s just…”
He hesitates a bit before continuing
- “I want to ask you a favour”
- “What kind of favour?”
- “That radio you have, it belonged to a friend of mine. Sheila was her name.”
“She disappeared on a food run a couple of months ago. That campfire you found? It’s pretty close to her old route...which is the first clue I have found in a while.”
“You know the city you just came from? Her hideout is just next to the edge of the city’s outer parts, somewhere east from here. She could have gone there...”
He pauses a bit
- “...Could you go there for me?”
- “I know it’s a lot to ask and I know it’s a long shot, but...what if she’s there?”
“Maybe she’s hurt or can’t move, or…”
Axel trails off, not finishing his sentence. He takes a deep breath and continues.
“Even if she’s not there...there is something I would like to get. To remember her…”
You listen quietly.
Changing paths like this had not been part of your plan when leaving the center of the capital, and the thought of it makes you anxious. Going by yourself too long in the wild is never a good idea.

On the other hand...not having anything to remember your loved ones by is something you can relate to. Your mother died when you were very young, and to this day you regret not having any pictures or possessions of hers. At times you can hardly remember her face...
- “Alright, I’ll go”
- “You will?!”
Excitement rings in his voice.
- “Thank you, really. It means a lot”
His words fill you with warmth. To have someone depend on you like this is something you haven’t felt in years. It makes you feel confident...useful.

choices.text: It’s a feeling you have missed.

content.text: - “So where do I go? You mentioned east.”
- “Yeah it should be just past that hill, do you see it?”
You turn east and look into the distance. For a moment all you can see is a small, grass covered hill, but looking closer you finally see a few rooftops hiding behind it’s edge.
- “Yeah I see it!”
- “Go through that city part, and the hideout should be on the other side!”

choices.text: With brisk steps you head towards the city

content.text: Compared to the center of the capital this outer part is quite small. You think it used to be called Potsdam. It probably won’t take more than an hour or two to walk across.
Like everywhere else this part is also covered in greenery, with thick green vines stretching across the various houses along the streets.

You walk along the grass filled sidewalk for a while when a thought suddenly hits you. Since you would be walking a bit longer than expected, it wouldn’t be such a bad idea to stock up on a few extra things.
Though the signs are quite torn, you can make out the names of a couple of different stores. Dry-cleaning, a few shoe stores and a jewelry shop. Nothing interesting.
Further down you see something you’re not quite familiar with. Lasertag? What the hell did they sell in there? Whatever it was, it’s probably not worth your time right now.
There’s got to be something around here!
Finally! You spot something useful. Several, in fact
A supermarket, an electronic store and an outdoor store.
You probably shouldn’t waist to much time in town, so there is probably only time to look inside one of the places. But where should you go?

On the road to your left you see a supermarket. It looks like it once was raided with several windows smashed and a wrecked entrance. People probably went there to steal food a long time ago. But there’s not only food in supermarkets. There should still be plenty of other stuff overlooked in there.

On your right, an electronic store peeks out from behind a mess of vines. It’s hard to make out the inside condition, but a few extra batteries would not hurt for the journey ahead. Maybe you could even tweak your hunting equipment a bit.

In front of you, you can see a big outdoor store. This one have signs of break in as well, so there might not be any guns or similar stuff left. But you should still be able to find a number of different survival equipment and other useful things.

The supermarket is probably your best bet you decide. Trying to avoid as much of the broken glass as possible you jump in through one of the windows. The inside of the supermarket turns out to be bigger than you expected, and most of the space further in is too dark to see.
With a bit of flickering the flashlight lights up the space. It’s doesn’t reach very far, but now you can at least see a bit in front of you as move further in.
“Abigail? Are you there?”
The sound of the radio startles you, and you drop the radio on the floor.
“Hello?”
On your knees you fumble in the darkness after the radio, finally finding it next to a shelf.
“I’m here, I’m here! Geez don’t scare me like that”
“Good, you had me worried there for a second. Have you made it through the city yet?”
“No not yet, I made a quick stop to get some supplies”
As you say that you notice some batteries on the shelf where you dropped the radio. You stick them in your pocket and
“Re-supplying huh? Alright. Just don’t take too long ok?”
“Good. I don’t mean to rush you, I just need to know if she is really there you know? I can’t think about anything else right now”
You feel bad for him and decide to quicken your search, not wanting to drag out your stay to long. As you browse along the shelves you realize that you don’t really know anything about this
“There’s no rush, but...I just need know if she is there. There’s to many ‘what ifs’ in my head right now”
You feel bad for him and decide to quicken your search, not wanting to drag out your stay too long. When browsing along the shelves you realize that you don’t really know anything about this
“How did you meet? You and Sheila?”
At first he doesn’t say anything, and for a moment you regret your question. Maybe it was too personal? After a few seconds, to your relief, you hear him
“It was many years ago. I was just a teenager back then, an arrogant and overconfident one at that. There was a shortage of food, and I had wandered far from my home to hunt.

I remember finding a deer herd. Killing just one of them would have been enough food for weeks. But in my excitement I approached them too quickly and they started running. As I ran off to follow them, my foot got stuck on a root and I fell hard down a steep hillside. I passed out, and when I woke up...there was ”
The way he says her name is reminiscent...sad. But there was another feeling in there, something you couldn't quite place. Maybe...anger?
He has been quiet for quite some time now.
“Axel?”
“Hm? Oh sorry I got lost in my thoughts”
You decide you must have been imagining things as he picks up where he left off earlier.
“She took care of me, helped me get back to where I came from. I will always be grateful to her because of that. After we got back, she stuck around”
He seems to ponder this for a while.
“In a way, yes. Being much older than me she became like mother figure to me. She was there for me when my dad died.”

The mentioning of his dad makes you remember your earlier conversation on the way out of the city. You start to wonder when he died, and how long it was just him and Sheila. Your own dad had died years ago, and after that you basically had to take care of yourself….
He seems to ponder this for a while.
“Yes and no. She became my teacher, and after my dad died more like a guardian to me.”

The mention of his dad makes you remember your earlier conversation on the way out of the city. You start to wonder when he died, and how long it was just him and Sheila. Your own dad had died years ago, and after that you basically had to take care of yourself….

Axel bursts into laughter. Taking deep breaths he makes a great effort to calm himself to answer your question, failing miserably. You have no idea why he thought this was funny. Should you be insulted? Justin never laughed at you like this.
Finally he gets a hold of himself.
“Definite no on that one. If anything she was more of a mother figure to me, as she was much older than I. She stuck around with me after my dad died”
You think to yourself how you ever got so bad at guessing, and quickly try to move on from the topic.

The mention of his dad makes you remember your earlier conversation on the way out of the city. You start to wonder when he died, and how long it was just him and Sheila. Your own dad had died years ago, and after that you basically had to take care of yourself….
Alex’s voice turns somber as he answers you.

“I miss him everyday. He always knew what to do, no matter what. Once he’d decided something he would stick to it, refusing to budge! Probably one of the most stubborn people I’ve ever met, even though that might not mean that much nowadays”
He stops his reminiscing, and turns his attention to you.
“I’m guessing your parents are not alive either? Being on your own and all.
“My mother died when I was quite young, and my dad died about 10 years ago.”
“What were they like?”

“He was very kind. Always looking out for me and wanting the best for me. I remember he once got an old phone to work with a bunch of music on it, which he gave to me even though he loved music himself. There was this high pitched pop song on it, and he teased me to death when I sang along to it. Apparently I am the most tone deaf person left alive he said.”
Thinking of this makes you smile.
“ I miss him...”
“He sounds like a fun guy” Axel says gently. “Hey! Now that I know that, I’ve got to hear you sing sometime!”
“Over my dead body!”
You both laugh together, which makes you feel much better after the earlier heavy topic.
In the corner of your eye you see stocked shelf and move further into the supermarket.

“She was a very technical person. We used to invent all kinds of stuff together by picking things apart and putting them back together. I eventually got pretty good at it, working a lot with generators and stuff like that. But I remember once when I went rogue and decided to put a heater together all by myself. It was a complete mess and it...sort of blew up. Almost gave my mom a heart attack! But even though I totally screwed up, she was proud of me for trying. She even let me keep my little failure. I called him Boris, since he was from Russia.”
Thinking of it makes your laugh to yourself.
“ I miss her...”
“She sounds like a cool lady” Axel says gently. “Hey! Maybe you can make a Boris for me to? I’ve always wanted an explosive pet!”
“You bet! I’d probably kill us both”
You both laugh together, which makes you feel much better after the earlier heavy topic.
In the corner of your eye you see stocked shelves and move further into the supermarket.

Most things you see are pretty useless, most things have either molded beyond recognition, or simply have no use in the world anymore. You’re almost about to give up when you see some plastic containers.
It’s

The amount is probably enough to last your whole journey. The plastic container worries you a bit, as your dad often warned you that water stored in plastic for too long could be unhealthy. But seeing as they have been lying someplace dark and cool you decide to risk it and place the water in your bag. You start moving towards the exit.
“Axel? I think I’m pretty much done here!”
“Great. Did you get everything you need?”
“Abigail?”
Abigail, do you hear me?”

You can hear him just fine, but you don’t dare to answer. In front of you is a big, moving silhouette. You freeze, not daring to move an inch.Then, slowly, you raise the radio towards your mouth and whisper.
“It’s a bear. There is a bear in here”

choices.text: “It’s a bear. There is a bear in here”

content.text: “What!? Oh shit! Has it seen you?”
The bear has moved close enough to your flashlight’s light for you to see the reflection of its eyes.
“It’s staring right at me!
- “Can you see the exit?”
You look over shoulder. It was only a few meter away from you. You take a deep breath.
“It’s alright, we can do this together. Move slowly towards the exit if you can.”
You do as he says, backing away slowly towards the exit. The bear’s attention is fixed on you.
“It’s looking at me Axel, what do I do??”
“Stay calm. You need to show that you’re not a threat.”
“How??”
“Just talk to me in a calm, low voice. And whatever happens DO NOT run”
You start to navigate your feet towards the door without stepping on anything that could make a noise. Suddenly the bear takes charge.
It’s running straight at you
“It’s gonna attack!”
“Keep going!” Axel urges “Remember; Do. Not. Run!”
You fight every instinct in your body as the bear run up to you at full speed. The second it reaches you
It sniffs you for a few seconds and with one last look it moves away, seeming to have lost interest.
You hear Axel’s whispering voice through the radio.
- “ Are you safe?”
- “Yes. I think it’s leaving”
- “You’re doing great. I am here with you.”
With a few steps backwards you reach the exit and without any hesitation, you run.
You run and run, out of the supermarket, out of the street and climb up a tree. You burst into tears, still terrified.
- “I’m okay, I ran out as soon as I reached the exit” Your voice is shaky and probably revealing that you are crying.
- “Are you hurt?”
- “No, just a bit shook. I thought I was a gonner there for a second.”
- “I think it was testing you. You handled yourself good out there.”
He sighs in relief.
- “I’m glad you’re okay. I’m really looking forward to meet you, you know.”
As he says that you can’t help to bawl as you are struck with a warm feeling that you are actually not alone

You feel excited when you read the name of the store since you instantly imagine all the things you could build if only you found some electric goodies. It feels really weird being inside the old store where everything is darker than outdoors.
“Hey, Axel. Do you mind keeping me company when I’m checking for stuff inside this old store?”
“Sure! What are you looking for?”
“Electronics, its just that it’s kinda… dark in here.”
“Oh, no problem”, he giggles.
“Heyy, it just feels nicer with company okey? It’s not like I’m scared heh, I laugh fear in the face!”
You both laugh, which makes you feel

You can start to make out what you’re looking at as your eyes are adjusting to the darkness. You can't find any super cool things. You do however, find some more batteries and and a few SD cards and some circuit boards that could come to good use.
You keep talking to Axel while
- “Well, this was disappointing, no cool stuff at all!”
- “Too bad, but I am pretty cool right? Hahah, just joking”
- “Heh, but actually it feels really nice to have someone keeping me company while feeling uneasy.”
- “yeah, I like you more and more you know. You are actually pretty decent and funny”
You feel butterflies in your stomach. How do you respond to this? Good thing he can’t tell that you are
- “haha aww, are you blushing??” he laughs
Ahh! How does he know??
- “Noo! I am just.. focused, heh.
Seriously Abigail, even though this is the first human you talked to in forever (no offence, justin) you hardly know him, butterflies already?? You think to yourself.
- “Are you still there Abby? Helloo?”
- “Yeah, I was just thinking, sorry.
- “it’s fine, I hope I didn’t say anything that made you upset? I was just trying to give you a compliment”
- “Yeah, sorry. It’s just been awhile with since I had proper conversations with anyone so I guess I’ll have to work on that part.
You eventually engage in a deeper

He tells you a bit about his mom and dad and you tell him about your family. It’s like it’s more real when you tell someone about them and hear their reactions to your mother's quirkiness and sigh at the things both of your dads used to do. It feels almost as though you have known eachother your whole life when you can relate to so much of what he is telling you.
“It would be really nice to live with other people again” you say.
He just answers with “did you find anything of value yet?”
You ignore his question and
- “you know, to be able to help out and talk about lots of things and never having to feel, well, alone and stuff.”
- “There would probably be a lot of problems too, now did you find anything or what?”

Now his lost the compassionate and cute tone in his voice. You get very confused about this reaction. Maybe he felt like you were trying to push that you wanted to move in with him? You feel embarrassed and don’t mention it again.
After that the conversation turns stiff and both of you become quiet. You have now made your way further back into the

All of a sudden you see a dark silhouette brush up against a shelf in the back of the room. You freeze. It is a big silhouette and you can hear a grunting. Slowly you raise the radio towards your mouth and whisper
- “Axel, I, there is, it's a bear!! I'm stuck in a back room”
- “What!? It’s alright, we can do this together. Has it seen you?, move slowly towards the exit if you can.”

You start to navigate your feet towards the door without stepping on anything that could make a noise. The bear haven't noticed you yet. With just a little bit left you decide to dash out the door. As you do, you hear a loud
The bear has noticed you. You can hear the massive body crashing through the shelfs and interior in the room behind you.
- ”What do I do!? It saw me!!” You yell into the radio this time.
- “Stay calm, is there any cabinet you with a door that you would fit into? Or something you could hide underneath?”

You throw yourself over a counter and find a big machine of some kind, it has a round hole in the middle with a hatch attached to it. With no time to think you decide to squeeze yourself into the hole and pull the hatch so that it’s as closed as you can get it.
You hear the bear entering the
You hear Axels whispering voice through the radio.
- “ Are you safe?”
- “Yes”
- “Now, just stay quiet and calm, don’t move. I am here with you.”
His words calms you down a bit even though you are still terrified.

You hear the bear sniffing on the hatch and your entire body tense up. You don’t want to die, not alone and definitely not in some old rusty machine. You don't know for how long you have been lying there when you notice that you can no longer hear the bear.
You push the hatch just a little to

The bear seems to be gone, you move slowly until you are sure and then you make a run for it. You run and run, out of the store, out of the street and climb up a tree. You burst into tears as this whole thing was terrifying. You radio Axel
- “I’m okay, I ran out as soon as I couldn’t hear the bear anymore” Your voice is shaky and probably revealing that you are crying.
- “Are you hurt?”
- “No, I am just shaken I guess. I thought I was a gonner there for a second.”
- “I am relieved to hear that you are okay. I am really looking forward to meet you, you know.”
As he says that you can’t help to baul as you are struck with a warm feeling that you are actually not alone

Yeah, this is probably the best bet. The floor is covered in broken glass as you enter the store, carefully so you don't step in to much of if.The store is much darker than outside, but as your eyes get used to the light you can tell that there are some good things in here, still unscavanged. Wonder why there are so much stuff left behind in here.
Good thing you brought the flashlight, you think to yourself as you slowly make your way deeper
The flashlight sparks alive at once, but after a few seconds it slowly dies. Wierd. You bang it against you palm a couple of times and turns it on again. Thankfully, it stays lit this time.
- “Hey Abigail, are you there?”
The sound of the radio catches you of guard, causing you to drop it in surprise. Dammit woman, how easily scared can one be?
- “Hello?”

You bend down to look for the radio, and you find it in a pile of what can only be rat droppings. Pretending that it’s chocolate sprinkles instead, like you’ve seen on pictures before, you pick it up with a shudder.
- ”Yeah, I’m here. God, you gave me such a fright I dropped the radio… Oh that's nice of you, quit laughing at me!”* you answering him, feeling yourself
- “Sorry, didn’t mean to startle you”, he says giggling.
Somehow you feel like you don't’ believe his sincerity.
- “Anyway, how's it going? Have you made it out of town yet?”
- “No, not yet. I found some stores so I thought I would check if there was anything good in there.”
After a short silence, he answers
- “Just don’t take too long, ok?”

choices.text: “I didn’t realise there was such a hurry.”

content.text: - “Sorry, I don’t mean to rush you. But I just need to know if she’s still out there, you know.”
It must be hard to lose a friend like that. You decide to hasten your search.

While browsing the shelves of the store you find what must be the entertainment section. While musing to yourself what it must have been like to live before the war, you find an object you’ve never seen before.
It’s called a… Selfie… Stick? What the hell is a selfie? The device has some kind of retractable function, but you are lost about what it could have been used for.
Thinking it must be good for something, you put it in your bag.
As you continue your search, you realize you don’t know anything about this
- “So how did you two meet? You and Sheila?”
At first he doesn’t say anything, and for a moment you regret your question. Maybe it was too personal? After a few seconds, to your relief, he
- “It was many years ago. I was just a teenager back then, an arrogant and overconfident one at that. There was a shortage of food, and I had wandered far from my home to hunt.

I remember finding a deer herd. Killing just one of them would have been enough food for a week. But in my excitement I approached them too quickly and they started running. As I ran off to follow them, my foot got stuck on a root and I fell hard down a steep hillside. I passed out, and when I woke up...there was
The way he says her name is reminiscent, and sad in a way. But there was another feeling in there, something you couldn't quite place. Maybe… anger?
He’s been quiet for a while now.
- “Axel?”
- “Hm? Oh. Sorry, I got lost in my thoughts”
You decide you must have been imagining things as he picks up where he left off earlier.
- “She took care of me and helped me get back to where I came from. I will always be grateful to her because of that. When we got back, she stuck around.”

choices.text: Was she part of your family?

content.text: - “In a way, yes. As she is older than me, she became like a mother to me. Or was older, I guess.”
You feel like he has something more to say, so you stay quiet while waiting for him to continue. When he talks again, his voice is thick with sorrow.
- “She was really there for me when my father died, you know.”

The mention of his dad makes you remember your earlier conversation on the way out of the city. You start to wonder when he died, and how long it was just him and Sheila. Your own dad had died years ago, and after that you basically had to take care of yourself…

choices.text: Do you miss him? Your dad?

content.text: - “Yes... and no. She became my teacher, and after my dad died, more like a guardian to me. But we were close, yes.”

The mention of his dad makes you remember your earlier conversation on the way out of the city. You start to wonder when he died, and how long it was just him and Sheila. Your own dad had died years ago, and after that you basically had to take care of yourself…

choices.text: Do you miss him? Your dad?

content.text: - “Hah! God, no. If anything, she was more like a mother figure to me, or a guardian. After my dad died. You see, she was much older than me.”
As he tries to calm himself after laughing at your question, you think to yourself how you ever got so bad at guessing.

The mention of his dad makes you remember your earlier conversation on the way out of the city. You start to wonder when he died, and how long it was just him and Sheila. Your own dad had died years ago, and after that you basically had to take care of yourself…

choices.text: Do you miss him? Your dad?

content.text: “Every day.” he answers quietly.

“He always knew what to do, no matter what. Once he’d decided something, he would stick to it, refusing to budge! Probably one of the most stubborn people I ever met, even though that might mean too much these days.”
He stops reminiscing and turns his attention to
- “I’m guessing your parents are not alive either? Being on your own and all”
- “My mother died when I was quite young, and my dad died about 10 years ago.”
- “What were they like?”

- “He was very kind. Always looking out for me and wanting the best for me. I remember he once got an old phone to work with a bunch of music on it, which he gave to me even though he loved music himself. There was this high pitched pop song on it, and he teased me to death when I sang along to it. Apparently I am the most tone deaf person left alive, he said.”
Thinking of this makes you laugh to yourself.
- “I miss him...”
- “He sounds like a fun guy” Axel says gently.
- “Well now that I know that, I’ve got to hear you sing sometime!”
- “Over my dead body!”
You both laugh together, which makes you feel much better after the earlier heavy topic. In the corner of your eye you see more stocked shelves and decide to

- “She was a very technical person. We used to invent all kinds of stuff together by picking things apart and putting them back together. I eventually got pretty good at it, working a lot with generators and stuff like that. But I remember once when I went rogue and decided to put a heater together all by myself. It was a complete mess, the thing almost blew up and would have destroyed our camp. Luckily mom stopped it in time! Even though I totally screwed up, she was proud of me for trying. She even let me keep my little failure. I called him Boris, since he was from Russia.”
Thinking of this makes you laugh to
- “I miss her...”
- “She sounds like a cool lady” Axel says gently.
- “Hey! Maybe you can make a Boris for me too? I’ve always wanted an explosive pet!”
- “You bet! I’d probably kill us both”
You both laugh together, which makes you feel much better after the earlier heavy topic. You notice that the store continues further behind two doors and decide to investigate further.
As you enter the area, you hear a crunching and rustling sound coming from somewhere behind you, but it was probably just the wind. You hope.
The people looting in here before you must have missed this area, as there are a lot of things left untouched.
Right ahead of you are some weapons and knives, which is always useful to have even though you hope to never use them.
To your left there are a whole bunch of utilities and stuff you’ve never seen before.
There is definitely some good stuff this way, not sure if I can find any batteries there though.
As expected, most of the knives have rusted away and all sorts of firearms are long gone.
This might be the safest bet to find something useful.
Eureka! Batteries! And it looks like they might fit the flashlight. Your discovery puts a smile on your face and you continue your search.
Once again you hear the crunching and rustling noise. You decide to ignore it, but you can't help feeling a shiver down your spine.
The joy is short-lived, however. As you search through the rest of the items, you realise that most of the stuff has either molded beyond recognition or has zero use in the world anymore.
You’re almost about to give up when you see some plastic containers.

The amount is probably enough to last your whole journey. The plastic container worries you a bit, as your dad often warned you that water stored in plastic for too long could be unhealthy. But seeing as it has been lying someplace dark and cool, you decide to risk it and place the water in your bag.
You start moving towards the exit.
- “Axel? I think I’m pretty much done here!”
- “Great. Did you get everything you need?”
- “Abigail?”
- “Abigail, do you hear me?”

You can hear him just fine, but you don’t dare to answer. In front of you is a big, moving silhouette. You freeze, not daring to move. Then, slowly, you raise the radio towards your mouth and whisper.
- “It’s a bear.”

choices.text: There is a bear in here.

content.text: - “It’s alright, we can do this together. Move slowly towards the exit if you can.”
You do as he says, backing away slowly towards the exit. The bear’s attention is fixed on you.
- “It’s looking at me, Axel. What do I do?”
- “Stay calm. You need to show that you’re not a threat.”
- “Just talk to me in a calm, low voice. And whatever happens, DO NOT run.”
You start to navigate your feet towards the door without stepping on anything that could make a noise. Suddenly the bear takes charge.
- “It’s going to attack!”
- “Keep going!” Axel urges.
- “Remember, do. Not. Run.”
You fight every instinct in your body as the bear runs up to you at full speed. The second it reaches you...
It sniffs you for a few seconds and with one last look it moves away, seeming to have lost interest.
You hear Axel’s whispering voice through the radio.
- “Are you safe?”
- “Yes. I think it’s leaving.”
- “You’re doing great. I am here with you.”
With the last few steps backwards, you reach the exit and without any hesitation, you run.
You run and run, out of the store, out of the street, and climb up a tree. You burst into tears as this whole thing was terrifying.
- “I’m okay, I ran out as soon as I couldn’t hear the bear anymore.”
Your voice is shaky and probably reveals that you are crying.
- “Are you hurt?”
- “No, just a bit shook. I thought I was a goner there for a second.”
- “I think it was testing you. You handled yourself well out there.”
He sighs in relief.
- “I’m glad you’re okay. I am really looking forward to meeting you, you know.”
His comforting words cause your feelings to overflow. You cry quietly to yourself for a while, feeling glad that you are not alone anymore.
You take a few moments to pull yourself together, as Axel gently supports you through the radio.
- “Feeling better?”
- “Yeah, I’m ok now.”
You look at the sun’s position in the sky and sigh.
- “This exciting resupply run took a bit longer than I expected. Better get moving.”
- “Are you sure? We can wait a few minutes if you want.”
- “No, it’s fine. I just want to get out of here.”
You pack up your things and start to move out of the city.
To your relief there is no sign of the bear anywhere as you exit the city border. You would not like another encounter like that again.
You are now faced with yet another forest, this one with taller and thicker trees than the previous ones.
“Where do I go now?”
“Just walk straight ahead. It should be close by.”
“On it!”
You start walking into the forest when Axel stops you.
“Abigail”
“Yeah?”
“...Be careful, ok?”
“I will.”
You keep your eyes open as you walk along the trees, keeping an eye out for anything that might be considered a hideout. Nothing really stands out until you reach
- “Axel, I think I’ve found it.”
In the cover of wild, spreading bushes and closely growing trees is a wooden cabin. From the look of it, you guess that it used to be an old hunting cabin.
- “Go and check it out. Maybe you can see through the window if she’s there?”
As you get closer, you notice that the windows are covered up, with no way to see what’s inside.
With no other option, you walk up to the cabin door. You consider what you should do.
You put your ear against the door and listen. It’s completely quiet.
You’re not sure if this is because of the thickness of the door, or if there’s just no sound coming from the inside. Either way, you’re left just as clueless as before.
You knock on the door carefully.
There’s no answer.
You try knocking on it one more time, a bit more determined this time.
Still no answer. Maybe there’s no one in there?
You pull on the door handle. The door is not locked, and you slowly pull it open.
“...Hello? Sheila?”
Because of the covered windows, the cabin is completely dark. There is a strange, foul smell in the air. Like old... meat? It’s almost hard to breathe.
You turn on your flashlight
Spread out inside the cabin are three people. Dead. From the look and smell of it, they have probably been dead for months.
You run out of the cabin and fall to your knees, hurling. Hyperventilating, you wrap your arms around yourself.
- “Abigail? How does it look? Is she in there?”
Your hands shake as you bring the radio to your mouth. After three deep breaths, you answer him.
- “Axel. There a-are dead people inside!”
- “I-I opened the door and there were three bodies in there. I... I don’t know what to do.”
The radio goes silent. You anxiously wait for him to respond.
- “Axel?”
No response.
- “Axel, please. I don’t know what to do!”
Finally, he answers you.
- “Are you ok?”
- “...I don’t know.”
He hesitates a bit before continuing.
- “I know it’s a lot to ask... but I need you to go back inside.”
- “Please, it’s important. I need you to get something inside the cabin.”
- “No, no I can’t.”
- “I swear it’s the last I’ll ask of you. I’ll explain everything later!”
You really don’t want to go, but you’ve come to trust Axel. And if he has reasons for wanting you to go back in there, it must be important, right?
You take three deep breaths, and once again
Flashlight in hand, you slowly enter the cabin.
- “Ok, what do I need to get?”
- “First of all... does any of the people look like they could be Sheila? She would be a woman in her 40s with black hair.”
Thankfully, you can answer him without looking closer at the bodies.
- “No... they’re all men.”
Axel turns quiet for a while.
- “Ok. In the room, does it look like there is a silver box anywhere?”
Just looking around the room, you can’t seem to spot any box, though there are several places where you could search for one.
There’s a bookshelf on your right filled with books and various items. Next to the bookcase is also a desk, which looks to have drawers you can open.
On the floor near one of the bodies is a blanket, which could potentially have something underneath it. Further into the cabin, you also see a half-open backpack lying in a corner.
Finally, looking up, you can see a few shelves that are just tall enough for you not to see what’s on them.
You fumble around the bookshelf, moving various books and old decorative items around. No silver box in sight.
The desk drawer is a bit hard to open, but with a bit of force you are able to pull it out.
You can’t see any silver box, but you do find something else. A picture?
In it is a boy in his late teens and a middle-aged woman with long black hair. Could this be Sheila? And maybe… Axel?
Either way, this is not the time to think about it, and you decide to move on.
You lift the blanket and jump back in fright as a huge rat runs out from underneath it and out the cabin door. No silver box here.
As your flashlight lights up the contents of the backpack, something glimmers back at you. You have found it!
- “I think I have found the box.”
- “Good, take it with you and get out of there!”
You give a sigh of relief, not wanting to stay in this place any longer than you have to. After putting the box in your bag, you move towards the door.
With the help of one of the chairs, you get a better look at the upper shelves.
They are extremely dusty, and you cough from the rising particles. Aside from a few old magazines, there is not much else to see.
You start looking elsewhere.
As you walk past one of the bodies on the way out, you notice something.
A symbol on one of the men’s jackets. A bird? The same kind of bird that’s painted on your radio.
You turn to the other two and see that they too have this symbol on their jackets.
Looking at them, you also notice another thing: a note, partially sticking out from one of the men’s pockets.
You pick it up. It might give you a clue to who they were.
The cabin is warm and the stench from the dead bodies is overwhelming.
You run out of the house.
Once you get out, you don’t stop, but keep walking. You just want to get as far away from this as possible.
Suddenly, you hear Axel’s voice.
- “Abigail, are you-”
- “I’m gonna find a place to rest.”
You firmly cut him off, which seems to catch him off guard.
- “Um, of course, whatever you need. Walk south and find a place to rest, then we’ll talk, ok?”
You don’t answer him but simply keep walking. Everything you have seen is starting to catch up with you, and you have no idea how to handle it.
You reach a little grove that looks like a good resting spot to recover from recent events. You are trembling and not sure how to start processing this.
You take a deep breath to gather your thoughts and to calm down.
Why did the dead men have the same bird symbol as Sheila’s radio? And why were they dead? And in her hideout nonetheless.
You remember the box Axel asked you to retrieve and take it out.
You look at the small silver box.

The box is not very big as it fits in your hand. It is made out of silver, or at least coloured in silver. There are no inscriptions of any kind, only a symbol, and the symbol doesn't tell you much. It is probably just there for decoration purposes.

As you open it, it reveals a key. A very normal-looking key without any symbols or anything on it. Odd. Perhaps it is a heirloom of Sheila’s or something that meant a lot to her, and that is why Axel wanted to have it retrieved.
You take out the note you found again.
The paper is all crumpled and dirty.
You unfold it to read it.
Small silver box. 10 cm * 15 cm. Inside the box is a dark grey key. The key is of great importance. And then there is a sketch of the key.
Why would they have a description of the box and key?
Your thoughts start to spin around in your head. This can’t be a coincidence, can it?
Sheila was obviously not there since it was three male bodies. Was she the one who killed them?
You decide to ask Axel about the symbols.
- “Axel.. When I was in there. With the, you know. Bodies. I noticed that they had the same symbol as the radio. What does it mean?”
The radio stays silent for a while.
- “Erh, I don’t know. Maybe she met some people out there and they decided to decorate their stuff with the same thing? Anyway, where are you now? And are you really okay?”
- “Yeah, I think I’m just a bit shocked.”
- “Understandable. It is getting late in the day and you should probably get a move on. I know a great spot on the way to make camp.”
You still want to get as far away from that place as you can, so you decide that you might as well get a move on.
If you keep walking, you will reach Axel before dawn.
You just want this weird and so far terrifying journey to come to an end.
You can gather your thoughts on the way. It's not like you would have been able to relax and sleep anyway.
The worst of the shock is gone and it feels better when you are on the move again.
You start to think about the key and the paper note again. It certainly doesn't feel like a coincidence like Axel suggested.
You haven't met people in years and now you had seen three dead in one day. How did they find her and how come they were dead? And what is up with this key?
You decide to try to talk to Axel about it again.
- “Axel? You there?”
No answer.
It is getting pretty late by now so you decide to try to radio him again in the morning.
You keep walking and thinking, and walking.
Your thoughts are interrupted by a string of light that hits your eye.
The sun starts to peek over the horizon. You must have been walking for hours already.
The warm red light starts to spread across the landscape, like someone poured out a bucket of sunlight.
You should be tired by now but you have never felt more awake.
You want to figure out what that was that you saw yesterday. You try to radio Axel again.
- “Axel, are you there?”
- “Good morning! Wow, you’re up early!”
- “I told you I was gonna walk all night. I wouldn't have been able to sleep anyway. So.. I have been thinking.”

choices.text: What do you think those men were doing there?
It is an odd coincidence that they had the same bird symbol, right?
What do you think happened to those three men and your friend?

content.text: “I have no idea, Abigail. It could have a thousand explanations. Maybe they teamed up and then had a fight, which resulted in what you saw yesterday?”
- “Well, if she liked that bird image, maybe she created it as a logo for them too? I don’t think that is of any importance, Abigail. It’s just a dumb drawing.”
Hm, maybe you are overthinking it? Or is he not telling you something?

- “That is a good question, she just stopped answering one day. Maybe that is when she met those guys. Maybe they were trying to trick her and that is when they started to fight, which resulted in what you saw yesterday. I really hope they didn’t do anything to her.”
- “Hm, yeah, perhaps. But I wonder how they died? I don't hang out with a lot of people but I mean, isn’t that a little extreme for a disagreement?”

- “Well, it depends on what kind. If they were trying to hurt her, I guess she didn't have a choice. But I don’t know what kind of people would do such a thing. Or maybe they just killed each other and Sheila wasn't even there.”
What could have happened.. That still leaves a few other questions.
And what about the key, you think to yourself.
There is no way that it could have belonged to one of the guys if Axel knew about it. It seems as if they were all after that key.
Perhaps it’s best to ask Axel about it without sounding too suspicious.

choices.text: It was a pretty box, that box of hers. What is it?
You know what else is weird? I found a note with a description of the key on one of the bodies..

content.text: - “Oh, it’s just something that she always had. I think she got it from her parents. It meant a lot to her so I didn't want it to be lost.”
- “What’s it for? I mean, does the key go anywhere in particular?”
- “Oh, you checked out the inside of the box. And no, not that I know of. Whatever that key went to is probably long gone.”
- “So it couldn't be of any importance to anybody else then?”
- “No, I find that very hard to believe. It is just sentimental value. And it’s probably best not to think about it. We will never know.”
He seems annoyed when you ask about these things.
Maybe he’s just feeling sad about Sheila and therefore feels uncomfortable talking about this? You decide to tell him about the note.
- “One of the guys seemed to be looking for the key, that's why I ask. He had a note on him.”
Now Axel seems even more upset.
- “Sheila probably just drew the key for her memoirs or something. There is no way that someone else would be after it.”
Axel answers very fast;
“Are you sure? It was probably just her notes of it in case she ever lost it or something. Why else would anyone bother to draw the key on a piece of paper.”
You did not tell him what the note said or what it looked like. This can only mean that he knows about the note. And that is why he has been acting weird when you asked him.

Your eyes tear up. You have enjoyed getting to know another person, the only person you talked to in years and a person that you started to think of as a friend. Perhaps you even had small hopes that it could become even more than a friendship one day.
You look up to wipe your tears as you notice a building a little further ahead.
You have reached the destination.
You stop. You just stand there for a while, crying.
- “Helloo? Abigail? What is happening? Are you ok? Why aren't you answering?”

choices.text: I didn't tell you about the sketch of the key on the note..

content.text: - “Why would I lie to you? What's going on?”
Your voice is shaking. You really don’t want this to be happening.
- “Do you swear that you didn't know anything about the guys or the note?”

- “I-, Abigail. There is something I want to tell you. I had nothing to do with those guys but I was trying to find Sheila because she stole that key from me. I just didn't tell you because I didn’t want you to think that I was using you to get to the key, because that is not true. I am sorry I didn’t tell you from the beginning. I just wanted to see who you were before I trusted you. I am sorry!”
You stay silent as you try to process this information. You are not even sure you know this person at all anymore.
- “Why did she steal the key? And what is the key for?”
- “Abigail, you have to understand that I was only trying to protect my home from outsiders by being careful. We have a water reserve that-”
You interrupt him;

- “.. yes, we. We are a group of people that have been hiding and living here for a few generations. It has always been important for us to protect the family from outsiders in case someone wanted to steal from us or hurt us. Abigail, I am so sorry but I couldn’t tell you until I knew you could be trusted!”

choices.text: Your sadness is now turning into anger

content.text: While you have been living alone all this time, there has been a group of people living here together?
- “When were you gonna tell me then??”

- “I-I’m not sure what I was thinking, it just became harder to tell you as more time passed. She stole the key to our water reserve and we are in trouble without it. Please understand, and please forgive me.”

You are not sure what to think. As you think back on how nice he has been and how happy it made you feel having someone to talk to, your anger starts to turn into sadness by the thought of losing that feeling.
- “That was just a guess! Abi-”
- “DON'T lie to me, I know you have something to do with this.”

- “I-, Abigail. There is something I want to tell you. I had nothing to do with those guys but I was trying to find Sheila because she stole that key from me. I just didn't tell you because I didn’t want you to think that I was using you to get to the key, because that is not true. I am sorry I didn’t tell you from the beginning. I just wanted to see who you were before I trusted you. I am sorry!”
All your trust in Axel is gone in an instant.
- “They wore your mark..” You say as you now stare at the bird in spray paint on the outside of the building.
- “I- They were never supposed to hurt her. Just retrieve the key. I take it you found your way here. Please let me explain. I really do want to meet you..”

- “This was all just something terrible that was never meant to happen. The key goes to our water reserve and we can’t survive without it. I had to protect everyone. Please, I want you to become a part of our family too..”

choices.text: You stand in front of the closed door that leads into the compound

content.text: You have so many thoughts and feelings bubbling. Who is this guy really? Was everything he said fake? What is the right thing to do?

choices.text: You chose to believe him, rather than living, perhaps, the rest of your life alone.
You can’t trust him, he was just using you to get the key.

content.text: - “Ok. I believe you.”
As you say the words, the door in front of you opens up from the inside.

You can’t believe what your eyes are seeing. Peeking back at you from the inside is a crowd of people. 20–25 men and women of different ages, some of them even kids. It’s more people than you’ve ever seen in your lifetime.
Suddenly you feel nervous. Was this really the right decision? Can you trust these people?
While you hesitate, a young girl comes up to you and says

choices.text: Are you the one who saved our water?

content.text: - “Uh well, I brought this?”
You take out the key from within your bag. Tears start to fill the girl's eyes, and without warning she throws herself at you in a fierce hug. It catches you off guard and you freeze.
This is the first time you’ve felt another human’s touch in...years. It feels so warm, so comforting.
- “I knew it! It’s true! You came to save us!”
More people dare to get closer to you now, also showering you with praise and affection.
- “I’m glad you got your water back, but you shouldn’t thank me. I came here for my own reasons.”
- “But you came”
That voice? Your eyes dart to the back of the crowd. A guy with short ruffled brown hair and dark brown eyes looks back at you, a relieved smile resting on his lips.
You both walk up to each other. Neither of you seem sure what to say.
Finally Axel speaks up.
- “...I’m really happy you decided to trust me”
- “I don’t”
- “Huh?”
- “I don’t trust you. Not completely anyway. In fact it will probably take me a while to completely trust you again”
Axel lowers his head as you continue.
- “But I’m willing to take that time...if I’m allowed to stay so”
He looks up at you, unsure if he heard right. Then he puts his arms around you in a tight embrace. Two hugs on the same day? You start to think you might be dead.
- “Of course you’ll stay! Stay as long as you want. And hopefully one day, I’ll earn your trust again.”
Then, in the midst of all these people, in the embrace of this man you barely know, you’re suddenly struck with a feeling. It feels like...home.
To answer him, you reach in your bag and pull out your radio. Before handing it over you pretend to speak into it one final time.
- “I’m looking forward to it”
“Goodbye Axel”
You drop the radio and the key on the ground and leave towards the setting sun.
You reach a vast field, with no landmark in sight. Staring at this empty scene you think; where will you go now?
Defeated, you sit down on the soft grass and bring out your bag. With the help of a few tools you easily start a camp fire and bring out your blanket-hammock to use as a pillow.
As you lie down in front of the fire, you see Justin staring at you from the top of your bag.
You lift out the poster and hold it in front of you, staring into the brown eyes printed on the paper. Tears start to fill your eyes.
“Well, wherever I’m going...I still have you”
You hug him close, as you fall to sleep...
